description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

I know it's not the first time, when templates are proposed ([1](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/1858), [2](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/1717), [3](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/1847), [4](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/1009), [5](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/90), [6](https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/1495)), but I couldn't find any existing issues related to local templates.

I know that [snippets](https://docs.zettlr.com/en/core/snippets/) were added, and I use them already, but they are global. It means I can use all templates in every directory that I open in Zettlr. But sometimes I have project specific templates that are useless for other dirs, so I think it can be useful to add local templates that are stored in the same directory where markdown files are stored. 

### Example Workflow

For example, I have a directory with lyrics of different songs. And I have a snippet with some structure that includes artist, language, translator (if it has), lyrics etc. And I'm sure that I will not use this template anywhere else.

Also, it can be useful for sharing directories/files because when you copy your directory, you copy not only .md notes, but templates too, making it easier to work together with other people or to send folders between different devices. 

If you back up your user data but not the app data itself, you'll still have your templates saved with your Markdown notes, even if you lose the application.

### Caveats

I don't know about them

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

I think it can be implemented with some "_configuration.json_" file that will be specific for each directory, that should include something like:

```json
{
    "templates": ["rel_path_to_temp_file", "rel_path_to_temp_file_1"]
}
```

When I want to create a new file, Zettlr should check if there are templates in this directory and propose to choose one of them (by default nothing is chosen and clicking enter without choice should create an empty file). 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c332c5a4-e5d0-4864-988a-3d6ca48543de)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c68a5386-1adb-4884-88d5-d2fdf9a61d6a)

Maybe templates can have a prefix "tmpl. " or something like that in their name and after a part of name that was given by a user

summary: feat: Add pause button to pomodoro timer.

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
Closes #5667 
This PR adds the option to pause and resume the pomodoro timer. It adds an additional button in the pomodoro popover.
## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
I added the button element to the PopoverPomodoro vue file as well as added a pause function and modifications to the the pomodoroTick function to enable the pause in App.vue. Style was added to maintain a spacing when both the pause/resume and stop buttons are displayed together. The start function also resets the paused to false so it doesn't retain the paused state when restarted. 

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->


<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version ->
Tested on: Arch Linux

summary: Zettlr fails to render a specific Mermaid diagram

description.text:
### Description

Only Zettlr cannot render valid mermaid block and other Markdown renderer like Marktext and other IDE integrated Mermaid preview in VSCode can render the diagram. 

In Zettlr, it says Cannot create file - undefined in red font. 

### Reproducing

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant A as Actor A
    participant B as Runner B
    participant C as Coordinator C
    participant D as Data Service D
    participant E as Processor E
    participant F as Engine F
    participant G as Analyzer G

    A->>B: Define pipeline
    B->>C: Submit pipeline
    C->>D: Fetch raw data
    D-->>C: Return data arrays
    C->>E: Apply processing steps
    E-->>C: Return processed output
    C->>F: Execute test run
    F-->>C: Return results series
    C->>G: Compute metrics
    G-->>A: Return report
```

```
sequenceDiagram
    participant A as Actor A
    participant B as Runner B
    participant C as Coordinator C
    participant D as Data Service D
    participant E as Processor E
    participant F as Engine F
    participant G as Analyzer G

    A->>B: Define pipeline
    B->>C: Submit pipeline
    C->>D: Fetch raw data
    D-->>C: Return data arrays
    C->>E: Apply processing steps
    E-->>C: Return processed output
    C->>F: Execute test run
    F-->>C: Return results series
    C->>G: Compute metrics
    G-->>A: Return report
```

Paste this, and it won't render on Zettlr. 

### Zettlr version

3.4.4

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

x64 exe Windows installer

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 10

### Additional Information

All other renders can render it correctly so it's Zettlr's problem.

### Description

I've noticed that the editor view scrolls or jumps unexpectedly while I'm typing. It feels like the text is constantly being re-centered or the focus is shifting, which disrupts the writing flow. I've tried adjusting some display settings and I closed a big file, but the issue still persists. I'm using Windows 11, and the Zettlr version is the latest: 3.4.4. Is this a known issue, or is there anything I can do to fix or work around this behavior?

### Reproducing

I just need to type a word and the editor view scrolls. 

### Zettlr version

3.4.4

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

Thanks a lot for your great work — I really enjoy using Zettlr! :)

summary: Bug: spawn EINVAL Error on Windows when Running yarn start

description.text:
### Description

While attempting to launch the Zettlr application on Windows after installing dependencies and starting the development server, the app consistently crashed with a spawn EINVAL error. The expected behavior is for the application to launch normally without throwing platform-specific errors during process execution. When launching the app, Node.js throws an exception from the child_process.spawn method, with the error shown in the screenshot. This originates from the script at: **

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ad88bb0d-cd70-42d8-b19b-b7449c84a0a1)

**

### Reproducing

To reproduce the issue, follow these steps:

**Clone the Zettlr repository on a Windows machine:**
git clone https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr.git
cd Zettlr

**Install dependencies:**
yarn install

**Start the application:**
yarn start

**Observe the crash with the following error in the terminal**
Error: spawn EINVAL
errno: -4071

### Zettlr version

"version": "3.4.3"

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Feature Request: Inline Word Definition and Thesaurus Popup

description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

I’d love to see Zettlr include an inline word definition and thesaurus popup feature. The idea is simple: when a user right-clicks or hovers over a word in the editor, a small popup or tooltip would appear showing a definition and optionally a few synonyms.

This would enhance the writing experience by helping users:

- Expand their vocabulary
- Avoid word repetition
- Quickly understand unfamiliar terms

It aligns perfectly with Zettlr’s writing and academic focus and would offer significant value to writers, researchers, students, and editors.

### Example Workflow

1. The user is writing or editing a document and wants to refine their language.
2. They right-click or hover over a word 
3. The meaning of the word appears along with synonyms that could be used instead
4. The user optionally clicks a synonym to replace the word or continues writing with enhanced clarity

### Caveats

Distraction risk: If implemented as a live hover feature, it could become distracting. To mitigate this, it could be activated only on right-click, keyboard shortcut, or deliberate toggle.

Privacy: If using an online API (like dictionaryapi.dev), users may be concerned about sending words over the internet. A good solution would be to:
1. Allow opting in/out of the feature
2. Support offline/local dictionaries in the future



### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

Yes

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Additional Information

Potential API: https://dictionaryapi.dev/ (free, no auth needed)
Mockup:
(Visual: Right-clicking a word opens a small tooltip with definition + synonyms)

This feature is inspired by tools like Grammarly, Notion, and Google Docs, which offer inline writing assistance — it would be a clean, powerful addition to Zettlr’s editor without cluttering the UI.

## Description

This PR allows to use shortcuts to navigate search results as in Visual Studio Code.
See video:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5760a6df-c066-4a77-9d76-e86147d5f2d0


## Changes

Before search results needed the mouse to be selected, now not anymore.

## Additional information

none

Tested on: Ubuntu 22 LTS

### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

Hi, I use Zettlr in a context where it is crutial not to loose any part of the notes taken.
So I need to have kind of a lock for each paragraph and to only be able to edit if it is unlocked

### Example Workflow

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/471add53-8d46-4883-bb9e-6d7a6a615a8b)

User wants to edit the first paragraph, so the Locked text/icon is clicked:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a5cca270-7907-4425-8f9d-c4403af3584d)

I would also propose to lock/unlock the current paragraph through any shortcut, any suggestion which one to use, e.g. Ctrl+L?

### Caveats

empty

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

Yes

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

_No response_

Description

Fix issue https://github.com/Zettlr/Zettlr/issues/5571 . Users can now open the next and previous active tab via Ctrl+PageUp and Ctrl+PageDown like in Visual Studio Code.

Changes

Shortcut to open the next and previous active tab is changed to CTRL+PageUp/Down as in Visual Studio Code.

Test

1. Open 3 documents
2. Press the shortcut Ctrl+PageUp
3. See that the next open document is shown
4. Press Ctrl+PageDown
5. See that the previous open document is shown


Expected behavior: the next/previous tab is shown.

Tested on: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS

### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [x] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [x] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

Introducing a Writing Streak Tracker that records the number of consecutive days a user writes in Zettlr and visually displays their streak history using a simple calendar or heatmap view. This feature encourages habit formation, improves consistency, and motivates users through visual feedback.

### Example Workflow

**Automatic Tracking Begins**
- A user opens Zettlr and begins writing. If they write at least 100 words (or a customizable threshold), the app logs the day as an "active writing day".- 
- The streak tracker updates automatically — no manual action required. 
**View Streak Panel**
- In the sidebar or toolbar, the user clicks a new icon labeled “Streaks”.- 
- A calendar heatmap (like GitHub’s contribution graph) shows days they wrote, with darker colors indicating higher word counts.
**Stay Motivated**
- On day 7, they write again — their current streak becomes 7 days.- 
- A small celebration message appears. 
**Streak Reset (If Missed)**
- If the user skips a day, the streak resets, and the heatmap shows the break clearly. 
- This visual feedback encourages them to write again and rebuild their streak.

### Caveats

- Introducing a streak calendar, stats, or notifications may conflict with Zettlr’s minimalist, distraction-free writing environment. 
- Tracking writing activity over time might require storing user metadata or logs. 
- Users in different time zones might experience inconsistent streak tracking, especially if writing late at night or traveling.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

Yes

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Additional Information

**Streak Tracker Panel (Sidebar or Tab)**
A new sidebar tab or collapsible widget titled “Writing Streaks” shows

**Calendar Heatmap View**
Inspired by GitHub contribution charts, this grid visually represents each day of writing activity

**Settings / Customization**
Options in preferences or context menu
Minimum words to count as a writing day (default: 100)
Toggle writing streak notifications (on/off)
Clear or reset streak history

**Custom Goals:**
Users can configure what counts as a “writing day” (e.g., 50, 100, or 500+ words).

**Project-Specific Tracking:**
Streaks could optionally be tracked per project/folder (e.g., “Dissertation Streak: 9 days”).
